title: Demo GRE Mathematics Subject Test Set
description: A short demonstration bank with representative single-choice questions. Full-length banks should contain 66 questions.

questions.question_markdown: Let $p(x) = x^3 - 4x^2 + x + 6$. How many distinct real roots does $p(x)$ have?
options: It has infinitely many real roots.
questions.solution_markdown:
Factor the polynomial:

$$p(x) = x^3 - 4x^2 + x + 6 = (x-2)(x-3)(x+1).$$

The roots are $-1$, $2$, and $3$, so there are three distinct real roots.

questions.question_markdown:
Let

$$A = \begin{pmatrix}1 & 2\\ 0 & 3\end{pmatrix}.$$

Which of the following is the determinant of $A^2$?

questions.solution_markdown:
Let $u = x^3$, so $du = 3x^2 \, dx$. The bounds remain $u=0$ to $u=1$, giving

$$\int_0^1 e^u \, du = e - 1.$$

questions.question_markdown: How many 4-element subsets of $\{1,2,3,4,5,6,7\}$ contain the element $7$?
questions.solution_markdown:
If $7$ must be included, choose the remaining 3 elements from the other 6 numbers:

$$\binom{6}{3}=20.$$

questions.question_markdown: Which statement about the sequence $a_n = \frac{(-1)^n}{n}$ is true?
options: It diverges because it alternates in sign.
Its absolute value diverges to infinity.
questions.solution_markdown: Since $\left|a_n\right| = \frac{1}{n} \to 0$, the sequence itself also tends to $0$.
